description: Use allium:elicit to discover and formalize sequential declaration-order execution semantics in the Allium spec. Produces the semantic decisions that Phase A (Phase 5 single-pass) and Phase B (repo-in-sequence) will implement.

produces.spec/common-repo.allium: Updated with sequential execution rules and CompositePrecedence presence guarantee (or explicit decision to keep silent drop)
produces.spec/detailed/operators.allium: Created fresh; specifies Phase 5 filter scoping and per-operator sequential semantics
produces.spec/detailed/auto-merge-composition.all: Updated with MergeLocalFiles interleaving rule and sequential repo: resolution semantic

session_startup: Run git status - verify on feat/sequential-operations-* branch
Read context/current-task.json and this plan file
Find first task where status=pending and blocked_by=null
open_semantic_questions: Q1 (repo-in-sequence): When repo: appears mid-sequence, does it merge sub-composite inline, or run its own full pipeline against current FS? How do its deferred merges execute?
Q2 (include/exclude interleaving): Can a later include re-add files that a prior exclude removed? Source: original-FS vs current-FS?
Q3 (rename chaining): Do mappings within a single rename op chain? Do sequential rename ops chain?
Q4 (CompositePrecedence presence): Can filters silently drop upstream composite content, or must presence be preserved?
Q5 (merge source removal): When a preceding exclude removes a merge's source, is it an error, warning+skip, or silent skip?
Q6 (MergeLocalFiles interleaving): Formal rule for merges and filters interleaving in declaration order.
Q7 (scope of sequential): Applies to self:, source:, and with: clauses. Does NOT change cross-repo ordering.

tasks.name: Read current Allium spec files end-to-end
tasks.description: Build a complete mental model of the current spec before eliciting changes. The elicit session will surface contradictions and gaps; knowing the current state makes those easier to catch.
steps: Read spec/common-repo.allium in full
Read spec/detailed/auto-merge-composition.allium in full
Read spec/detailed/merge-operations.allium in full
Note: no spec/detailed/operators.allium exists yet (will be created)
Summarize: existing entities, rules related to ordering, rules about Phase 5, rules about CompositePrecedence (lines 770-785 in main spec)
Note any rules that will need modification or removal when sequential execution is codified
acceptance_criteria: Agent has read all three existing spec files
Mental model includes: what ordering rules exist today (cross-repo DFS post-order), which rules constrain Phase 5, which rules are silent on declaration order
List of rules likely to need modification is in agent's working context

tasks.name: Run allium:elicit session with user to resolve semantic questions
tasks.description: Invoke allium:elicit skill. The session is a structured conversation with the user to resolve Q1-Q7 (see open_semantic_questions above). Agent's job: prepare questions, drive the conversation, capture decisions, produce draft spec rules.
steps: Invoke Skill tool with skill=allium:elicit
Prime the session with the seven open questions (Q1-Q7) from this plan
Reference the concrete bug scenario from context/sequential-operations-context.md so decisions are grounded in a real case
For each question: elicit the user's intent, push back on vagueness, capture the rule

Specifically for Q1 (repo-in-sequence): clarify what 'current FS state' means when repo: is mid-sequence — is it the accumulated filesystem from preceding ops, or does the sub-repo run in isolation and get merged in?
Specifically for Q2: establish whether include re-adds from source FS or only from current FS (only current FS is implementable without keeping a source-FS copy around)
Produce draft rule text for each semantic decision
tasks.output_file: spec/common-repo.allium (draft updates held by elicit session)
acceptance_criteria: All seven semantic questions have explicit decisions captured as draft Allium rules
Decisions are consistent with each other (no contradictions)
Decisions cover the concrete bug scenario — user can point to which rule makes the scenario work correctly
Draft rules are ready to be written to spec files

tasks.name: Create spec/detailed/operators.allium
tasks.description: Create the new operators.allium file referenced but missing. Contains per-operator sequential semantics and Phase 5 filter scoping decisions from the elicit session.
steps: Use allium:tend to write the new file if elicit didn't already do so
Include: Include, Exclude, Rename, Template, TemplateVars operator specs
For each operator: in-place modification vs new-FS semantics, source-FS vs current-FS behavior, chaining rules
Reference and link to decisions from run-elicit-session
acceptance_criteria: File exists at spec/detailed/operators.allium
Each operator has an explicit sequential semantic rule
Q2 (include/exclude) and Q3 (rename chaining) decisions are encoded
File references back to common-repo.allium for shared entities

tasks.name: Update spec/common-repo.allium with sequential execution rules
tasks.description: Add/modify rules in the main spec for within-repo sequential execution and CompositePrecedence presence guarantee.
steps: Add a WithinRepoSequentialExecution rule (or equivalent) stating YAML order = execution order for all operations within a self:/source/with: block
Update or extend CompositePrecedence (lines 770-785) per Q4 decision
Add a rule for merge source removal (Q5) if the decision warrants spec-level codification
Ensure rules compose with existing cross-repo ordering (depth-first post-order from Phase 3) — sequential applies WITHIN a repo/block; DFS applies BETWEEN repos
acceptance_criteria: Within-repo sequential execution rule exists
CompositePrecedence reflects Q4 decision
No contradictions with existing cross-repo ordering rule
Allium syntax is valid

tasks.name: Update spec/detailed/auto-merge-composition.allium
tasks.description: Codify MergeLocalFiles interleaving and sequential repo: resolution in the auto-merge spec.
steps: Update MergeLocalFiles step 6 (or replace steps 5 and 6) to specify merges and filters interleave in declaration order (Q6)
Add a rule for repo: resolution at declaration position (Q1) — specifying what happens to the sub-repo's composite and its deferred merges when repo: is mid-sequence
Ensure MissingSourceAutoMerge rule still holds; extend or reference Q5 decision
acceptance_criteria: MergeLocalFiles reflects interleaving
repo-in-sequence rule exists and is testable
Q1 and Q6 decisions are encoded

tasks.name: Validate all spec files parse and are internally consistent
tasks.description: Run Allium validation / use allium:tend or allium:weed to verify spec files are well-formed and internally consistent.
steps: Invoke allium:tend with query to validate/normalize all three spec files
If validation errors, fix them (tend will guide)
Check for cross-file reference errors (entity referenced in one file but not defined anywhere)
Verify decisions from run-elicit-session are all reflected
acceptance_criteria: All spec files validate cleanly
No undefined entity references
Q1-Q7 decisions all present and consistent across files

tasks.name: Trace the concrete bug scenario through the updated spec
tasks.description: Sanity check: walk through the bug scenario from context/prompt.md step-by-step, applying the new spec rules. Verify the spec predicts the desired outcome (merged .pre-commit-config.yaml contains both local and upstream content).
steps: Take the concrete YAML config from context/prompt.md (the self: block with include/template/rename/repo)
For each operation, state which new spec rule governs its execution
Trace the FS state at each step: initial empty → after include → after template mark → after template-vars → after rename → after repo: resolution
Confirm the final FS contains .pre-commit-config.yaml with the merged content
If the trace doesn't produce the expected outcome, the spec is wrong — return to run-elicit-session
acceptance_criteria: Trace written down (in context/operations/ or similar)
Every operation maps to a spec rule
Final FS state matches the 'Expected' outcome from context/prompt.md
If mismatch: spec revised and re-validated

tasks.name: Revise Phase A plan to match the final spec
tasks.description: The current context/sequential-operations.json (Phase A plan) was written before the spec discovery. Reconcile it with the final spec — some tasks may need updates, especially around Q5 (merge source removal behavior) and the existing merges-before-filters test semantics.
steps: Re-read context/sequential-operations.json
For each task, check its acceptance criteria against the new spec rules
Adjust test expectations that were based on guessed semantics (e.g., Q5 decision may change what test_phase5_sequential_exclude_before_merge_removes_source asserts)
Update docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-sequential-operations.md to match
acceptance_criteria: Phase A plan's test assertions match the Q5 decision from elicit
Phase A plan's acceptance criteria reference specific spec rules where applicable
Reference markdown plan is synced

tasks.name: Expand Phase B plan skeleton into detailed tasks
tasks.description: The Phase B plan is currently a skeleton. Now that the spec defines repo-in-sequence semantics (Q1), write concrete tasks for the architectural change.
steps: Read context/phase-b-repo-in-sequence.json
Using Q1's decision, write detailed tasks covering: how repo: is identified mid-sequence, how the sub-pipeline runs, how its composite merges into the parent FS, how deferred ops propagate
Include E2E test task that exercises the exact concrete bug scenario from context/prompt.md
Include code review and verification checkpoints as in the Phase A plan
Decide if this needs to be broken into sub-plans (may be large enough to warrant the plan-to-plan pattern from CLAUDE.md)
acceptance_criteria: Phase B plan has concrete tasks, not skeleton placeholders
Tasks reference specific files to modify (orchestrator.rs, discovery.rs, processing.rs)
E2E test against the bug scenario is included
Review checkpoints are in place

tasks.name: Commit spec changes and plan updates
tasks.description: Commit Phase 0's artifacts: updated specs, new operators.allium, revised Phase A plan, fleshed-out Phase B plan.
steps: Run ./script/ci to verify prose linter passes (specs are subject to prose rules if they contain markdown-style text)
git add spec/ context/
Commit with conventional message: 'docs(spec): formalize sequential declaration-order execution semantics'
Include note that code changes come in Phase A and Phase B
acceptance_criteria: ./script/ci passes
Commit contains all three spec files, both updated plans, and the context doc
Commit message is conventional and descriptive

tasks.name: Archive Phase 0 plan and activate Phase A
tasks.description: Phase 0 is complete. Move this plan to context/completed/ and update current-task.json to point to Phase A.
steps: git mv context/phase-0-spec-discovery.json context/completed/
Edit context/current-task.json: plan = 'context/sequential-operations.json', description reflects Phase A focus
Commit: 'chore(context): archive Phase 0 (spec discovery), activate Phase A (Phase 5 single-pass)'
acceptance_criteria: context/phase-0-spec-discovery.json is in context/completed/
context/current-task.json points to context/sequential-operations.json
Commit created

notes: Entry point for fresh sessions: context/sequential-operations-context.md
The elicit session (run-elicit-session task) is the critical task — all downstream tasks depend on its decisions
If elicit reveals that Phase A or Phase B scope needs to change (e.g., they need to be merged, or split further), update the plans during revise-phase-a-plan and flesh-out-phase-b-plan
Phase 0 does NOT modify any .rs code files — only specs, plans, and context docs
If spec-against-bug trace fails, don't hack the spec; return to elicit with the failure case
